laws.title: Utah Minimum Wage — $7.25/hr (Federal Rate)
laws.summary: Utah does not set a state minimum wage above the federal rate. All covered employers must pay non-exempt employees at least $7.25 per hour under the federal Fair Labor Standards Act. Tipped employees may receive $2.13/hour provided tips bring total compensation to at least $7.25/hour.
compliance_actions: Pay all non-exempt employees at least $7.25/hour
For tipped employees, ensure employer-paid wage plus tips equals at least $7.25/hour
Post the federal FLSA minimum wage poster in a visible workplace location
Retain payroll records for at least 3 years
